title: Thelypteris lawakii
intro: Thelypteris lawakii is a species of fern, specifically a member of the Thelypteridaceae family, native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris lawakii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Thelypteris lawakii is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris lawakii is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns. Thelypteris lawakii is further classified as a species of the subfamily Thelypteridoideae. The subfamily Thelypteridoideae is part of the order Polypodiales, which comprises various species of ferns. The classification of Thelypteris lawakii is also associated with the class Polypodiopsida.
